---
title: "<i>Magazine for the Young</i>"
---

## <i>Magazine for the Young</i>

**Type:** periodical

### About

This periodical was founded by Mary Anne Dyson and her sister-in-law and published by James Burns, but edited from soon after the start by Anne Mozley (1809–1891), whose publisher brothers took over its publication in 1850; it ran 1842-1875. It was known in CMY’s circle as ‘the Pink Mag’;  it was a tiny duodecimo production with a pink paper cover.   CMY published in this periodical  ‘Chapters on Flowers’ (February 1850-December 1852), which later appeared as <i>The Herb of the Field </i>(1853); twenty-seven ‘Langley School’ stories (September 1846-December 1848), fifteen of which appeared as <i>Langley School</i>; ‘Mrs Elderney’s School’ which was never reprinted in volume form (January 1850-Jan 1852); ‘Thoughts on Pictures’ (1853), also never republished; <i>Friarswood Post Office</i> (August 1858-August 1859);  <i>Countess Kate</i> (October 1861-December 1862); and <i>New Ground </i>(January 1863-November 1866).

### Letters Mentioning This Organization

- [To Christabel Rose Coleridge, Oct 19 [1898]](../letters/3386_To_Christabel_Rose_Coleridge.html)

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
